summary: In March 2026, Marin Ivezic comprehensively analyzed claims that RSA-2048 or RSA-4096 encryption has been secretly broken using quantum computing. He reports no credible evidence supports these claims. Publicly, the largest number ever factored is 829 bits; quantum computing’s record using Shor’s algorithm is trivial—only the number 21, or five bits. Breaking RSA-2048 would require about 100,000 to 1,000,000 physical qubits with error rates a billion times lower than today, while current quantum hardware has roughly 1,000 noisy qubits. Ivezic details that no verifiable public breakthroughs exist, and previous similar claims have been debunked. He urges organizations to follow National Institute of Standards and Technology post-quantum cryptography standards and ignore unverified vendor claims.
